name: Video Chess
description: Video Chess is a digital implementation of the classic board game Chess, designed for single-player and two-player gameplay. The game features a standard 8x8 chessboard and all the traditional chess pieces: King, Queen, Rook, Bishop, Knight, and Pawn for both white and black players. The game adheres to all standard rules of chess, including movement restrictions for each piece type, castling rules, en passant capture, and promotion of pawns reaching the opposite rank. The objective of the game is to checkmate the opponent's King. The game provides visual feedback to indicate legal moves for a selected piece. A game over state is triggered when either a checkmate occurs, a stalemate occurs, or a player resigns. A single-player mode allows the player to compete against an AI opponent with adjustable difficulty levels. A two-player mode allows two human players to compete against each other on the same device. The game tracks and displays the current turn, indicating whose move it is (White or Black). It also provides a clear visual representation of the chessboard and pieces, allowing for easy identification and selection.

controls.arrow_up: Move the cursor/selection up on the chessboard.
controls.arrow_down: Move the cursor/selection down on the chessboard.
controls.arrow_left: Move the cursor/selection left on the chessboard.
controls.arrow_right: Move the cursor/selection right on the chessboard.
controls.space: Select a chess piece or confirm a move to the selected square.
controls.q: Rotate the board's perspective for enhanced visualization (e.g., between a White-perspective and a Black-perspective).
controls.z: Undo the last move (if available, depending on the game mode and settings).
